titles.en: Chapter 10 – The intention in the swearing of the oath
Chapter 10 – The intention in the swearing of the oath

segments: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ وَ سُئِلَ عَمَّا يَجُوزُ وَ عَمَّا لَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ النِّيَّةِ عَلَى الْإِضْمَارِ فِي الْيَمِينِ فَقَالَ قَدْ يَجُوزُ فِي مَوْضِعٍ وَ لَا يَجُوزُ فِي آخَرَ فَأَمَّا مَا يَجُوزُ فَإِذَا كَانَ مَظْلُوماً فَمَا حَلَفَ بِهِ وَ نَوَى الْيَمِينَ فَعَلَى نِيَّتِهِ وَ أَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ ظَالِماً فَالْيَمِينُ عَلَى نِيَّةِ الْمَظْلُومِ .
arabic.text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ وَ سُئِلَ عَمَّا يَجُوزُ وَ عَمَّا لَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ النِّيَّةِ عَلَى الْإِضْمَارِ فِي الْيَمِينِ فَقَالَ قَدْ يَجُوزُ فِي مَوْضِعٍ وَ لَا يَجُوزُ فِي آخَرَ فَأَمَّا مَا يَجُوزُ فَإِذَا كَانَ مَظْلُوماً فَمَا حَلَفَ بِهِ وَ نَوَى الْيَمِينَ فَعَلَى نِيَّتِهِ وَ أَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ ظَالِماً فَالْيَمِينُ عَلَى نِيَّةِ الْمَظْلُومِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas’ada Bin Sadaqa who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying, and he<sup>asws</sup> had been asked about what is allowed and about what is not allowed from the intention on the conscience regarding the oath, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is allowed in one place and it is not allowed in another. So as for what is allowed, so when one was oppressed, so whatever one swears with and the core of the oath, so it is upon his intention; and when there was an oppressor, so the oath would be upon the intention of the oppressed’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas’ada Bin Sadaqa who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying, and he<sup>asws</sup> had been asked about what is allowed and about what is not allowed from the intention on the conscience regarding the oath, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is allowed in one place and it is not allowed in another. So as for what is allowed, so when one was oppressed, so whatever one swears with and the core of the oath, so it is upon his intention; and when there was an oppressor, so the oath would be upon the intention of the oppressed’.

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Harun ibn Muslim from Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when he (the Imam) was asked about what is lawful and what is not lawful about intention and what is in one’s mind, saying, ‘In certain times it is lawful and in certain times it is not lawful. The lawful case is when one is oppressed in which case his oath is based on his intention. If one coerces then it is on the basis of the intention of the oppressed.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Harun ibn Muslim from Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when he (the Imam) was asked about what is lawful and what is not lawful about intention and what is in one’s mind, saying, ‘In certain times it is lawful and in certain times it is not lawful. The lawful case is when one is oppressed in which case his oath is based on his intention. If one coerces then it is on the basis of the intention of the oppressed.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/322)
gradings.label: ضعيف
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/322)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَةَ بْنِ صَدَقَةَ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 10 – The intention in the swearing of the oath

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ حَلَفَ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ قَالَ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الضَّمِيرِ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ حَلَفَ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ قَالَ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الضَّمِيرِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ismail Bin Saeed Al Ashary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who swore and oath and his conscience was upon other than what he had sworn. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The oath is upon the conscience’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ismail Bin Saeed Al Ashary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who swore and oath and his conscience was upon other than what he had sworn. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The oath is upon the conscience’.

segments: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ’Isma‘il ibn Sa‘d al-Ash‘ariy who has narrated the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who swears but his mind is on something other than what he has taken oath for. He (the Imam) said, ‘The oath is for that which is in one’s mind.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ’Isma‘il ibn Sa‘d al-Ash‘ariy who has narrated the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who swears but his mind is on something other than what he has taken oath for. He (the Imam) said, ‘The oath is for that which is in one’s mind.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/322)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/322)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ الْأَشْعَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 10 – The intention in the swearing of the oath

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الضَّمِيرِ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الضَّمِيرِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Safwan Bin Yahya who said,
‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the man who swore, and his conscience was upon other than what he had sworn upon. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The oath is upon the conscience’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Safwan Bin Yahya who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the man who swore, and his conscience was upon other than what he had sworn upon. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The oath is upon the conscience’.

segments: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Safwan ibn Yahya who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who swears but his mind is on something other than that for which he has taken the oath. He (the Imam) said, ‘Swearing is for that which is in one’s mind.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Safwan ibn Yahya who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who swears but his mind is on something other than that for which he has taken the oath. He (the Imam) said, ‘Swearing is for that which is in one’s mind.’”
